extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won Stage 8 sprint after GAVIRIA Fernando was pinned against barriers by GREIPEL André in final 200m chaos when no lead-out trains survived.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE Julian absent from sprint lead-out after pacing back MARTIN Dan group post-crash, exposing GAVIRIA Fernando without Quick-Step train structure.
GREIPEL André following SAGAN Peter acceleration from 300m fractured all lead-out formations, turning sprint into pure freelance speed contest favoring GROENEWEGEN Dylan.
MARTIN Dan positioning 17km from finish on easy stage cost 1:14; sitting back to recover energy backfires when crashes occur inside 20km.
Day 8 fatigue breaking lead-out teams allows pure sprinters like GROENEWEGEN Dylan to beat tactically superior squads lacking fresh domestiques.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: consecutive wins reveal superior top-end speed when lead-outs collapse into chaos, improving form daily
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: twice fastest but without ALAPHILIPPE Julian supporting, lacks tactical positioning instincts to win without perfect Quick-Step lead-out structure
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: positive: physically immovable at 40mph holding left-hand barrier line, can follow accelerations but lacks terminal speed at Stage 8
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: negative: sitting too far back 17km out on easy stage shows poor risk assessment; crash cost 1:14 and revealed positioning discipline weakness
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: sacrificed sprint duties to pace MARTIN Dan back, showing team prioritizes GC protection over stage-hunting opportunities
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: accelerating from 300+ meters without speed to finish exposed tactical desperation, easily absorbed by GREIPEL André
